job.title: Industrial Cybersecurity Consultant (34053)
job.description:
Industrial Cybersecurity Consultant
STSI is looking for a Cyber Security Consultant who is detail oriented with a willingness to utilize their investigative mind and dig into remediation issues, conduct security audits, and implement change plans in IT & OT environments. Ideal candidates have experience assessing security and PLC controls with experience conducting security audits, maintaining up to date understanding of guidance from the U.S. Cybersecurity and Infrastructure Security Agency (CISA), and have a firm understanding and experience with Allen Bradley PLC and Modbus PLC universal communication protocol.

Type: Full-time Contract - W2 ONLY, accepting candidate that are a US Citizen (nonnegotiable)
Compensation: $ 50.00 USD to 60.00 USD
# of Positions: 3-5
Location: Richmond, VA and Glen Allen, VA
Travel: 80% - 100% - accepting only local candidates in the state of Virginia who are able to drive/go to different locations/offices in VA. Consultants will use their own vehicle and must have a valid driver's license.
Expense: Miles, Hotel (when required), food are reimbursed expenses.
Schedule: Daylight, Monday through Friday.
Reports to: Director of Network & Endpoint Security, Project Manager & Delivery Lead

Position Summary:
The Cybersecurity Consultant will independently execute significant portions of projects addressing Information Technology (IT) and Industrial Control System security. The Cybersecurity Consultant supports the execution of projects consisting of network penetration testing, web application security testing, cybersecurity vulnerability assessments, secure system design and integration, and/or development of cybersecurity programs at client sites across the U.S and Canada utilizing the National Institute of Standards and Technology (NIST) Cybersecurity Framework (CSF), NIST Risk Management Framework (RMF), and other industry or data specific compliance frameworks and regulations. Execute the planning, design, development and implementation of technical controls, procedures and policy associated with cybersecurity compliance and/or regulatory standards.


Responsibilities:
  • Maintain highest level of integrity, protecting the confidentiality and security of all clients and project information.
  • Identify and diagnose operational issues and implement design alterations to address these issues.
  • Conduct network penetration tests and vulnerability assessments of IT and Operational Technology (OT) networks, for both compliance and security purposes.
  • Perform detailed, post event analysis of unusual events, and direct needed procedure or process changes in response.
  • Pursue, obtain, and maintain industry recognized IT certifications related to cybersecurity such as ethical hacking, network engineering, Industrial Control System (ICS), Supervisory Control and Data Acquisition (SCADA), risk management, and others as necessary.
  • Resolve technical issues, analyze implications to the client's business, and be able to communicate them with applicable stakeholders within the business.
  • Develop policies & procedures for secure process control network design, technical and design recommendations for the implementation of firewalls and other network security and compliance controls.
  • Compiles technical documentation of network traffic as well as firewall services/solutions including explanations and diagrams.
  • Work collaboratively with other groups and divisions inside the company.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.
  • Comply with all policies and standards.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.

Required Experience:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Cybersecurity, Computer Science, Computer Engineering, Electrical Engineering, or a related technical field and min 3 years of related/relevant experience is Required.
  • Advanced knowledge of security principles and firm knowledge of cybersecurity technologies, as well as industry-recognized certifications.
  • Experience with cybersecurity vulnerability assessments, penetration tests, and the tools/techniques involved in both.
  • Experience in the capabilities and/or configuration of cybersecurity controls, specifically those relating to firewalls, access control, authentication, anti-virus/anti-malware, patching, and logging.
  • Advanced knowledge of control systems utilized by utilities, manufacturing, oil and gas, transportation, smart buildings, and cities.
  • Strong written and oral communication skills.
  • Strong analytical and critical thinking skills.
  • Ability to operate under pressure and under tight deadlines, to operate in on-site industrial, corporate, and government work.
  • Demonstrate capability to make sound decisions based on good security practices and principles.
  • Demonstrate an understanding of business principles and operational security practices specific to engineering and/or security consulting.
  • Knowledge and/or experience with corporate policies and procedures.
  • Strong technical writing skills. Knowledge and experience with modern and legacy computer networking and telecommunications.
  • Experience with physical cabling for network communications and control system Input/Output.
  • Ability to obtain and maintain access to current and future client sites.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Business Development Manager – IT Staffing Services (34025)
job.description:
Title: Business Development Manager – IT Staffing Services.

About us: Myticas Consulting Group of Companies. Myticas specializes in IT Staffing and Recruitment Services with over 50 years of combined experience in the IT Staffing industry. We serve clients in all industries in the United States and Canada with offices in Ottawa, Chicago, Atlanta, Cleveland, and Grand Rapids. 

About the position: The Sr. Business Development professional will sit in our Chicago, Illinois office This position is a hybrid role and requires some travel. This position is responsible for Business Development in this area as a priority, but able to develop business in OH, PA, WV, IN, IL, etc. This position comes with a competitive salary, commission, bonus potential, medical, dental, vision, 401K, and PTO. This professional would have full access to training, access to and support from the marketing department and technical tools in support of their success. 

Job Duties and Responsibilities:

  • Initiate contact, develop relationships, and conduct business development activities with clients and professionals; networking and connecting with IT professionals, executives, managers, and other professionals and resources at potential, new, and existing clients.
  • Research and qualify new clients, conducting business development activities and negotiating terms of service and agreements with new clients.
  • Provided expert level staffing support to clients, hiring managers, and professionals. Qualifying technical position requirements and coordinating with technical recruiters to fulfill staffing needs for new and existing clients.
  • Represent and market the  Myticas Consulting Group brands.
  • Participate, network at, support, and sponsor events involving the technical community.
  • Sell via telephone, face-to-face and videoconferencing.
  • Utilize Bullhorn CRM/Application tracking system for client and manager outreach and keep CRM documentation, agreements, resources, and paperwork up to date.

Required Experience:

  • 3-5 years’ demonstrated record of success selling staffing services, preferably with experience in the IT environment.
  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills.
  • Outstanding customer service, integrity, and interpersonal skills.  
  • Energetic, enthusiastic, self-motivated, collaborative.
  • Passion for the staffing industry, excellent work ethic, and strong desire to succeed.

job.title: Network Fiber Planning Engineer (34654)
job.description:
Fiber Planning Engineer
Conshohocken, PA (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $49.50 - $66.00

Description:
Our Transmission & Distribution global practice is looking for a Fiber Planning Engineer to work as part of the FirstEnergy Fiber Replacement Program (FRP) Team. This FRP team will help FirstEnergy manage a large-scale, multi-year portfolio of fiber rebuild projects, affecting topology of MPLS, SONET, and DWDM networks. In this role, the candidate will guide internal network architecture, fiber planning/routing, and fiber execution efforts.

Responsibilities:
  • Assess existing and future transport solutions utilizing design tools such as GIS. Transport solutions will be determined based upon location type, substation size, remote access requirements, etc.
  • Attend weekly client fiber project review sessions, participate in quarterly network planning, and implement coordination meetings.
  • Coordinate with GIS team on documentation of logical schematic and geographic network designs for systems including, but not limited to MPLS, DWDM, and SONET.
  • Work with network design engineering on integrating fiber designs into network design packages. Provide guidance to design team on transport solutions and network topology.
  • Coordinate FRP buildout plans with the FirstEnergy Portfolio Development lead to develop schedule and budgets.
  • Assist in the creation and submission of Fiber Optic Work Requests (FOWR), the review of fiber cost estimates, and quality review of fiber design packages. Review fiber scope & estimation proposals to ensure technical and constructability criteria are met.
  • Assist in the documentation of fiber initiatives and processes, including process documentation updates and deliverables to the client, “How To” guides, etc.
  • Participate in site visits to assess existing substation control house conditions and confirm new equipment needs. Additional investigation of distribution lines along fiber route may be required.
Requirements:
  • Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering or related degree from an ABET accredited program and min 3 years of electrical engineering experience (consulting preferred) is Required – OR – Bachelor Degree in Electrical or related Engineering Technology from an ABET accredited program and successful completion of Fundamentals of Engineering (FE) exam and min 3 years of electrical engineering experience (consulting preferred) is Required – OR – Master Degree in Electrical Engineering and min 2 years of electrical engineering experience (consulting preferred) is Required.
  • Experience with utility experience or fiber network design experience is required.
  • Must be a self-starter and highly motivated to continually learn new technical skillsets and grow as a network engineer.
  • Must have excellent written and verbal communication skills and strong analytical and problem-solving skills.
  • Must be capable of applying intensive and diversified knowledge of principles and practices to broad areas of assignments.
  • Strong organizational skills and the ability to manage a vast array of information.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.description:
Platform Engineer 
Chicago, IL (On-site)

Description:
The Platform Engineer will design, develop, and deploy complex infrastructure operating platforms and solutions while providing technical expertise to clients and internal teams. This position will create and implement platforms that manage and support various software applications and processes. 

Responsibilities:

  • Experience building cloud infrastructure in GCP. Prefer experience with IaC (Infrastructure as Code) tools like Terraform or Pulumi for provisioning resources.

Tempe, AZ (On-site)

  • Knowledge & application of NFPA 70 (NEC) and electrical design requirements.

Sr. Telecom BIM Designer
Tempe, AZ (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)       
Pay Range: $45.00 /hr. - $61.00 /hr.     

Summary: We're looking for a Telecommunications BIM Designer who is excited about working on projects that enable the heart of our clients’ business. Our BIM designers are engaged in the design documentation while being responsible for setup and management of Revit BIM models in coordination with other discipline BIM leads and team members.

Responsibilities:
  • Create and manage content towards fully coordinated 3D models across all design disciplines (C,S,A,M,E,P,T,I,L) as well as creating 2D construction drawings.
  • Coordinate with other designers, discipline leads, architects and engineers.
  • Work to turn the design intent of our senior telecom design team members into constructable deliverables including clash-free 3D models.
  • Responsible for compliance with company and site safety policies.
  • Performs other duties as assigned.
  • Complies with all policies and standards.
Qualifications:
  • High school graduate or equivalent.
  • At least 8 years of Revit BIM design experience.
  • Proficiency with NavisWorks.
  • At least 4 years of relevant experience in telecommunications systems design and construction industry.
  • Understanding of how codes and standards shape design decisions.
  • Understanding of design and construction specifications and their function.
  • Understanding of wiring/cabling connections in relation to a circuit or a link between devices and equipment.
  • Experience following client design and modeling standards.
  • Proficiency with Bluebeam or similar markup software.
  • Effective written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to estimate hours required for design and modeling efforts based on provided markups and/or verbal direction requests.

job.title: Lead AWS Operations Engineer (34565)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Chicago, IL, is currently seeking a Cloud Specialist (AWS) for a 1 Year contract position.

NOTE: Looking for W2 candidates.

Top Required Hard Skills:

Subject matter expertise: Demonstrated advanced experience integrating legacy platforms and applications with AWS.
AIOps proficiency: Strong experience with AIOps, including the ability to provide guidance, leadership, and automation solutions.
Production monitoring: Extensive background in production monitoring using tools such as Grafana, Dynatrace, Splunk, and CloudWatch.

Top Required Soft Skills:

• Problem-solving ability: Advanced capability to solve problems by analyzing available information, prioritizing tasks, and making timely decisions. This includes correlating data using standard business and technology tools, spotting trends, and applying sound security and risk management principles.
• Time management and prioritization: Exceptional skills in assessing necessary activities and prioritizing assignments. This requires excellent time management, prioritization skills, and a strong work ethic.
• Presentation and communication: Proficiency in creating and delivering presentations to both technical and business audiences, with an understanding of the differences in communication strategies required for each.

Job Description:
As Cloud Specialist (AWS), you will serve as a lead technical operations expert responsible for the design, implementation, and support of cloud-based information technology solutions in both current and future states. In this role, you will be tasked with building, maintaining, and operationalizing infrastructure, security services, and system components in a government cloud AWS environment to deliver highly available and scalable services. You will join an on-call rotation and are expected to provide expertise and technical leadership on any issues that may arise.
Our team is actively engaged in projects to migrate legacy applications to AWS, innovate in AIOps, and enforce a strong baseline for operational readiness. This role demands confidence, leadership qualities, professionalism, and integrity.

job.title: Sr Systems Engineer, On-Prem (34563)
job.description:
Myticas's direct client, based in Chicago, IL, is currently seeking a Software Engineer – III for a 1 Year On-site contract position.

NOTE: Looking for W2 candidates.

Top Required Hard Skills:

• Subject matter expertise in areas such as:

o PKI, LDAP, routers, firewalls, networking.

o Middleware solutions (e.g., Sterling Connect:Direct, IBM MQ, SecureTransport Server).

o Proxy solutions (Sterling Secure Proxy, Tivoli Access Manager) and client-side technologies (Axway SecureClient).

•  Ability to design and lead customer validation tests, develop use cases, and create detailed testing plans aligned with business and technical requirements.

Top Required Soft Skills:

• Strong skills in:

o Project management methodologies.

o Coordinating resources for testing and operational activities.

o Communicating technical concepts in business terms to executives and stakeholders.

•  Demonstrated capability to drive process improvements, manage complex customer problems, and lead customer calls for critical issues.

Job Description:
The Senior Systems Engineer Contractor leads the technical design and validation of client's solutions supporting national financial services and external organizations. This role manages technical labs, ensuring environments(hardware and software) remain current, secure, and ready for testing, validation, and certification activities. The position requires advanced technical expertise, strong leadership in cross-functional collaboration, and the ability to drive process improvements while maintaining compliance with client security standards. Ability to quickly triage major issues within application flows is vital.

PRINCIPAL DUTIES AND RESPONSIBILITIES:
• Technical Design & Validation
o Lead design and validation of Fclient's solutions.
o Identify gaps between current and future states; develop solutions to close those gaps.
o Design and execute customer validation tests, including detailed test plans and use cases aligned with security and compliance standards.
• Lab Management
o Oversee technical labs to maintain currency with evolving technologies.
o Ensure readiness for testing, validation, and certification activities.
• Testing & Analysis
o Coordinate technical and end-user resources to execute tests and analyze results.
o Document findings, communicate issues, and provide timelines to stakeholders.
o Assess labor and technical skill sets required for testing activities.
• Operational Support
o Implement customer-side monitoring tools.
o Lead operational outage events and manage complex customer problem resolution.
o Drive process improvement efforts for client channels.
• Performance & Standards
o Analyze client solutions for performance and cost optimization.
o Develop and maintain client;s standards; monitor SLA targets and ensure alignment with strategic objectives.

REQUIRED EDUCATION AND EXPERIENCE:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Computer Science, Engineering, MIS, or related field (graduate degree preferred).
  • 5–7+ years of direct technical work experience, including 2+ years in leadership roles within complex organizatio
  • Proficiency in PKI, LDAP, networking, routers, firewalls, middleware (Sterling Connect:Direct, IBM MQ, SecureTransport), proxy solutions (Sterling Secure Proxy, Tivoli Access Manager), and client-side technologies (Axway SecureClient).
  • Familiarity with multiple operating systems (Windows, Unix/Linux, Solaris).

Other Required Skills:
  • Advanced knowledge of IT testing methodologies, infrastructure and integration testing.
  • Strong understanding of project management methodologies.
  • Excellent analytical, problem-solving, and decision-making abilities.
  • Strong background in production systems 24/7 x 365 support
  • Ability to influence and lead cross-functional teams without direct authority.
  • Exceptional written, verbal, and presentation skills; ability to communicate technical concepts to non-technical audiences.

job.title: Technology Governance Lead (34731)
job.description:
Technology Governance Lead
Location: Kansas City, MO
Pay Range: Negotiable $71.00 - $93.00 per hour
Relocation: Available
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)

Summary

This role leads the design, implementation, and operation of enterprise technology and data governance within the security function. They will partner with the individual serving in the CISO role, the Chief Digital Technology Officer (CDTO), and key business stakeholders to ensure technology and data policies, decision rights, and controls align with business outcomes and risk appetite. The role owns program-level activities including technology and data inventory and mapping, classification and minimization, stewardship model, data access controls, data risk assessment, and governance metrics to support compliance, security, and AI readiness.

Responsibilities
  • Governance: Establish the rules, policies, and structures that direct how the technology and Business Enablement organization operates, ensuring accountability and strategic alignment
  • Risk Management: Guide enterprise cyber risk management practices and alignment with business risk tolerance
  • Compliance Oversight: Monitor adherence to external laws (e.g., GDPR) and internal policies to identify compliance gaps
  • Stakeholder Communication: Partner with Executive Leaders and senior stakeholders to deliver meaningful insights into the organization’s risk posture and compliance status.
  • Program Implementation: Lead the selection and deployment of GRC software (e.g., Vanta, MetricStream) to automate controls and reporting
  • Define and maintain the technology and data governance charter, scope, and decision-rights model (RASCI) in collaboration with business and organizational stakeholders
  • Develop and operationalize technology and data classification, minimization, and retention policies with business, privacy, and security partners
  • Oversee the development of a prioritized inventory of technology and data assets to enable discovery, classification, lineage tracking, and risk analysis
  • Lead stewardship program design and placement (centralized, federated, or hybrid), assign data owners and stewards, and embed stewardship into operational workflows
  • Design and run Data Risk Assessments (DRA / FinDRA) to prioritize datasets by business impact and to inform security investment decisions.
  • Serve as a primary liaison for external audits, regulatory reviews, and client-facing assessments to provide assurance and evidence for compliance activities
  • Specify controls and access governance (PBAC/Purpose/role-based) and coordinate with IAM, DSPM/DLP, and SIEM/SOC as needed
  • Select and oversee integration of governance tooling (catalog, lineage, DSPM, DQ, MDM, observability)
  • Implement metrics, dashboards, and reporting that link governance activities to business KPIs and risk metrics for executive stakeholders
Qualifications
  • Bachelor Degree in Computer Science, Information Technology or related field AND 8 years information security experience. 
  • Capability to define policy, decision rights (RASCI), and steward models (centralized/federated/hybrid)
  • Demonstrated ability to engage Executive Leadership, advance data literacy, convene steering committees, and translate risks into business and financial terms for senior leaders
  • Proven experience leading technology or data governance programs and working cross‑functionally to operationalize classification, stewardship, access controls, risk assessments, exception management, and continuous improvement
  • Experience conducting risk assessments and working with security, privacy, legal, audit, and business teams to translate risks into prioritized remediation and investment needs
  • Technical knowledge of metadata, data cataloging, lineage, MDM, data quality, DSPM/DLP, DataOps/Data observability concepts, and governance platform integrations with data architectures.
  • Applied experience in project management principles and methods
  • One or more professional cybersecurity certifications (e.g., Security+, CISA, CRISC, CISSP)
EEO/Disabled/Veterans

job.title: Lab Tech - Micro (34657)
job.description:
Lab Tech-Micro - Laboratory Testing
Contract: W2– Candidates must be authorized to work in the U.S. on a W2 basis. 
Duration: 6 months with possible multi-year extension/s
Pay: $20-22 per hour on W2 
Hours:  Monday-Friday, 40hrs, 8-5pm, On-Site
Location: Grand Rapids, MI; Considering local candidates only

Micro Lab Technician: Laboratory Testing
This is an entry level position for a Microbiology Lab Technician, which operates under the Quality Control Unit. In this role you will be supporting testing and release of raw materials, finished consumer goods, and/or environmental samples.

This first shift position will work as part of a cohesive laboratory team which often uses a rotational schedule to keep the work interesting and to ensure workload is covered. You will be trained on multiple techniques within the micro laboratory and will be kept up-to-date with team meetings. Daily duties may include cleaning, record keeping, media preparation, and calibration of equipment. Once trained, occasional weekend work may be required.

Skills to be successful in the role:
• Flexible team player
• Attention to detail
• Ability to communicate and elevate issues to leadership
• Ability to sit, stand, and lift up to 50lbs
• GMP microbiology laboratory experience preferred

Required qualifications:
• Laboratory experience (in education or industry)
• Demonstrated ability to follow SOPs/test methods

Education: 2 years of college in a science related field and/or demonstrated experience in microbiology laboratory procedures

job.title: SAP Technical Analyst (34615)
job.description:
Clover Consulting has a direct client in Grand Rapids, MI in need of an SAP Technical Analyst for a contract or contract-to-hire opportunity.  This positon will require on-site work in Grand Rapids, MI.  Candidates local or willing to relocate should apply.  

TITLE:  SAP S/4 Technical Analyst
TERM: 10+ Months Contract or Contract-to-Hire
TYPE:  W2 or C2C
RATE:  Negotiable
LOCATION:  Grand Rapids, MI - HYBRID work required (3-4 days/week On-Site)
Candidates willing to relocate will be considered

Technical Analyst
We are looking for a technical analyst with primarily SAP S/4 PPDS and/or PP manufacturing applied experience to contribute to the objectives of our global team and its ongoing projects. In this role, you will apply Agile methodologies to facilitate the efficient delivery of robust SAP S/4 technical solutions, that advance cross-functional business strategies and initiatives.
 
The team you will be part of    
 
We are a global team managing the migration from SAP ECC to S/4 to support manufacturing, distribution, and logistics. Our versatile members ensure smooth transition and ongoing ECC technical support, working collaboratively to achieve team objectives and align with company goals.
 
Primary Responsibilities
 
As a technical analyst, your responsibilities will include:
·         Interpreting functional specifications and translating them into implementable and supportable technical solutions
·         Developing technical specifications utilizing standardized templates
·         Creating and maintaining comprehensive technical documentation
·         Conducting research on S/4 platform best practices to address business process requirements
·         Collaborating with technical developers to ensure deliverables meet business requirements and expectations
·         Unit testing developed solutions
·         Troubleshooting business and technical processes on platforms such as SAP ECC, S/4, and .Net
·         Applying minor ABAP code enhancements and SAP configuration
·         Applying Agile methodologies and principles throughout the entire development lifecycle
·         Maintaining up-to-date knowledge of emerging technologies and proactively sharing innovative ideas for process improvement
·         Providing support for end-user requests or issues through the incident reporting tool, ServiceNow
 
Knowledge and Skills
Applied experience in S/4 PPDS
Applied experience in Discreet and MTO manufacturing
Applied experience with manufacturing BOMs and Routings
Applied experience with manufacturing production orders
Applied experience in PP a plus
Experience in SAP ECC 7 or above
Experience in maintaining SAP system configuration (SPRO)
Experience with Agile methodologies and concepts
Experience in debugging transactions, ABAP programs, and function modules
Experience in writing ABAP code a plus
Experience with Microsoft Office Suite, including Word, Excel, Visio, and PowerPoint
Knowledgeable in all aspects of ABAP development, including reports, enhancements, interfaces, function modules, workflows, and classes
Knowledgeable in Fiori, SAPUI5, CDS views, OData technologies, APIs, Cloud Platform, HANA DB, and implementing or extending standard SAP Fiori applications
Knowledgeable in BADIs, enhancement points, BAPIs, IDOCs, and Web Services
Knowledgeable with Azure DevOps (ADO) a plus
 
Who you are
 
·         Build strong relationships and communicate clearly and effectively with diverse team members
·         Demonstrate a strong work ethic, share knowledge, achieve goals, and meet deadlines
·         Apply solid problem-solving and analytical abilities
·         Adaptable, as team members must work in modules/areas of S/4 and ECC that they are not familiar with
·         Facilitate data-driven discussions and decisions
·         Support projects that enhance business capabilities
·         Tackle complex system challenges with intuitive thinking
·         Stay updated on system methods, emerging technologies, and best practices
·         Focus on serving the team, customers, IT, and Steelcase
·         Collaborate with cross-functional, global teams
·         Lead and teach others empathetically
 ​​​​​​​

job.title: Design Engineer- Wood Millwork Focus (34765)
job.description:
Design Engineer – Wood / Millwork Focus

Location: Wood Dale, IL (On-site)
Schedule: Monday – Friday, Onsite
Salary Range: $70,000 – $90,000

About the Company

Our client is a design and manufacturing organization that develops custom products and engineered solutions for commercial applications. The company combines engineering, product development, and manufacturing expertise to deliver high-quality products that meet customer requirements for performance, cost, and production timelines.

The team operates in a collaborative environment where engineering works closely with production and project teams to develop practical, manufacturable designs.

Job Summary

We are seeking a Design Engineer with experience in wood products, millwork, or cabinetry design to support the development of engineered products and assemblies. This role will focus on the design of wood-based components and structures, translating conceptual designs into detailed documentation that supports efficient manufacturing.

The Design Engineer will collaborate with internal teams to create manufacturable designs, produce engineering documentation, and ensure products meet quality, cost, and production requirements.

The salary range for this position is $70,000 – $90,000, depending on experience and qualifications.

Key Responsibilities

  • Design and develop wood-based components and assemblies for manufactured products

  • Create 3D CAD models and detailed engineering drawings

  • Produce assembly and installation drawings for manufacturing and production teams

  • Develop and maintain Bills of Materials (BOMs)

  • Collaborate with production teams to ensure designs support efficient manufacturing and assembly

  • Apply design for manufacturability (DFM) principles to improve product quality and efficiency

  • Support prototype development and testing when needed

  • Identify opportunities for cost reduction and process improvement

  • Work with cross-functional teams to support custom product development and engineering projects

  • Manage multiple design projects while meeting timelines and quality standards

Preferred Technical Experience

  • Cabinetry or architectural millwork design

  • Wood materials, panel products, and engineered wood components

  • Joinery methods and cabinet construction techniques

  • Lamination processes and surface finishing

  • Integration of hardware and multi-material components

Qualifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical Engineering, Industrial Design, Wood Product Engineering, or related field

  • 5+ years of engineering or product design experience in a wood manufacturing or millwork environment

  • Experience working with wood-based assemblies and multi-material products

  • Proficiency in 3D CAD software, preferably Autodesk Inventor or similar tools

  • Familiarity with AutoCAD or comparable drafting software

  • Experience working with ERP systems and BOM management

  • Strong communication, organization, and problem-solving skills

  • Ability to manage multiple projects in a fast-paced engineering environment

job.title: Turnaround Piping Designer (34784)
job.description:
Turnaround Piping Designer
Location: Billings, Montana (on-site)
Contract - 3 months / turnaround
Schedule: 5-10s, M- F: $125/day per diem
  • A minimum of 5 years of experience in design and drafting in a refinery, or equivalent, setting.  Ideally this would be someone that has worked at a client’s site, or at least has good experience working in industrial settings.
  • Preference would be for someone that has experience with the Client's  refinery.
  • Proficient in the use of Cadworx
  • Become familiar with and adhere to Client's Safe Practice Standards and Client's Corporate Safety Standards as needed.
  • Able to go outdoors into the refinery and wear PPE consisting of Fire-Resistant Clothing, hard hat, safety glasses, safety toe boots with a defined heal, and ear plugs.  This is Client's minimum PPE requirements.
  • Perform field walkdowns if needed for layout, reviews, and verifications for design work.
  • Familiarity with Process and Instrumentation Diagrams (PID) and refinery equipment.
  • Ability to perform extended walkdowns to verify as-built status of process equipment within various units in the refinery.
  • Generate field markups of as-found installations on existing piping and instrumentation diagrams for review with Operations Process Engineer.
  • Create field sketches of existing installations for generation of Process Flow Diagrams (PFD) and Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (PID) to document existing installations. Review field sketches with necessary SMEs and generate CAD files to document systems.

job.title: Staff Civil Site Engineer (34777)
job.description:
Staff Civil Site Engineer 
Norfolk, VA (On-site)

Benefits: Access to client benefits package 

Description: The Staff Civil Site Engineer will work on project teams to create world class site designs for new projects, alterations, and redevelopments on a variety of projects including pipelines, airports, institutional, industrial, manufacturing, municipal, commercial, and government facilities. 

Responsibilities: 
  • Perform/assist in civil design and calculations for projects from the conceptual phase through design completion.
  • Modify and review drawings for a variety of projects.
  • Design civil components for project needs and requirements that are set forth by the project managers, while incorporating client, local, state, and federal regulations including, but not limited to:
    • Site Demolition
    • Erosion and Sediment Control
    • Grading
    • Drainage
    • Stormwater Quality/Quantity
    • Site Layout
    • Sanitary Sewer Layout and Design
    • Domestic and Firewater Layout and Design
    • Utility Profiles
  • Apply strong knowledge of commonly used site civil engineering/design concepts, principles, practices, codes, and procedures within the site civil engineering services industry.
  • Research and compile project-related data as required by the project managers. Compile studies and reports. Write specifications.
  • Compile information for client presentations, shop drawing review, and contract administration.
  • Perform field inspections, measurements and/or calculations.
  • Perform other duties as assigned.
  • Mentor younger staff.
  • Comply with all company policies and standards.

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in civil engineering or related degree from an ABET accredited program and 3 years of civil engineering experience, consulting preferred – OR – Master’s degree in civil engineering and 2 years of civil engineering experience, consulting preferred.
  • Strong knowledge in standard civil engineering techniques and procedures.
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Ability to work methodically and analytically in a quantitative problem-solving environment and demonstrated critical thinking skills.
  • Strong computer skills (e.g. Microsoft Office Suite).
  • Strong computer skills (e.g. AutoCAD, MicroStation, BIM (Revit), Civil 3D, PLS, PLS-CADD, RISA-3D, Math CADD), and/or hydraulic, hydrologic, pavement, civil analysis programs.
  • Strong attention to detail, facilitation, team building, collaboration, organization and problem-solving skills.
  • Engineers in Training-EIT Certification Preferred.


We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Automation & Controls Engineer (34782)
job.description:
Automation & Controls Engineer 
On-site/Field Location(s): 
Atlanta, GA
Saint Louis, MO
Kansas City, MO

Benefits: Access to client benefits package 


Description:The Automation & Controls Engineer executes Automation and Controls Engineering design activities for minor to major scale Engineering, Procurement, and Construction (EPC) projects in food, consumer products, pharmaceutical, life science, aerospace and industrial facilities. Responsible for executing automation and controls deliverables for project(s) in the front end, detailed design, construction, and start-up phases. Projects range from controls projects working directly for the client to controls self-execution on full scale Automation and Controls EPC projects.

Responsibilities:
  • Responsible for compliance with company and project site safety policies.
  • Works with a team of Automation and Controls Engineers, Specialists, and Designers in the execution of design. Interfaces with a multi-faceted project team internally consisting of project manager, electrical, controls, estimating, procurement, construction, and other engineering disciplines.
  • Responsible for developing software design deliverables including PLC and HMI programming design deliverables as well as Batch, MES, servers and virtualization. Responsible for commissioning and startup of those design deliverables.
  • Develop controls design deliverables, including instrument/device list, I/O list, controls narratives, control panel and controls hardware design, device location plan drawings and installation details.
  • Develop electrical design deliverables including 480VAC and below equipment and motor power design, installation plan drawings, and conductor schedules
  • Develop the specification and design requirements for control panel and equipment vendors, track vendor progression, and lead the Factory Acceptance Tests (FATs).
  • Experience with process instrument measurements protocols, such as temperature, pressure, flow, level, analyzers, and control valves.
  • Utilize knowledge of various technologies for selecting the appropriate controls application.
  • Experienced in reviewing P&IDs and working with process engineers to define and show proper controls scope of the project.
  • Develop verification of design, calculations, project studies, design basis and report preparation.
  • Interfaces directly with offsite design office and coordinates and tracks execution of all electrical controls department work to that office.
  • Responsible for performing quality reviews of project design submittals.
  • Responsible for applying knowledge of commonly used automation and controls engineering/design concepts, principles, practices, codes, and procedures within the engineering services industry.
  • Interfaces directly with construction personnel in development of contracts and execution of work.
  • Tracks Automation and Controls design efforts against budgets and expected progress.
  • Works with clients and vendors directly in developing design and builds lasting business relationships.
  • Responsible for leadership and guidance of less experienced team members.
  • Coordinate with field personnel to resolve design related installation issues, calculations, field inspections and testing of controls systems.
  • Responsible for technical execution during onsite start-up and commissioning activities.
  • Develops and submits discipline change notices as needed to the project management team.
  • Understands the basics of the EPC project execution methodology.

  • Participates in interviews for early career team members.

  • Coordinates workload with Automation and Controls Department Manager.
  • Complies with all company policies and standards.
  • All other duties as assigned.     

Qualifications:
  • Bachelor’s degree in Controls, Electrical, Mechanical or Chemical Engineering or related degree from an ABET accredited program and successful completion of Fundamentals of Engineering (FE) exam and 3 years electrical engineering experience. EPC project and Automation 7 Controls experience; preferred - OR - Master’s degree in electrical engineering and 4 years’ electrical engineering experience. EPC project experience; preferred.
  • Expert knowledge in standard engineering techniques and procedures.
  • Strong knowledge of electrical equipment design ANSI and IEEE Standards.
  • Strong computer skills (e.g. Microsoft Office Suite).
  • Excellent written and verbal communication skills.
  • Experience with leading the discipline design for large projects and delegating work tasks to team members. Ability to lead execution of work and resolve issues in a team environment.
  • Demonstrated critical thinking skills, ability to work methodically and analytically in a quantitative problem-solving environment.
  • Strong attention to detail, facilitation, team building, collaboration, organization and problem-solving skills.
  • Ability to perform quality reviews for detailed engineering documents and specifications.
  • Ability to travel. accredited program.

job.description:
Assistant Electrical Designer 
Cary, NC (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $29.40 - $36.75 

Summary: An Assistant Electrical Designer will assist in various phases of planning, site evaluations, analytical designs, report preparation, specifications, and preparation of proposal and construction documentation and electrical plans as part of a project team. 

Responsibilities:
  • Assist in designing various electrical project designs including creating electrical schematics, grid plan layouts, electrical power systems, lighting systems, protection and controls, and site and facility design.
  • Assist with set up of electrical CAD configuration and procedures according to project requirements at the beginning of a project for the electrical design team.
  • Assist in preparing engineering drawings from rough sketches or from general engineering and design information (verbal instructions and notes) using CAD software programs
  • Assist with a full range of drawing production work including problem analysis, design layout and calculations on non-routine assignments of substantial variety and complexity.
  • Assist with a variety of assignments using prescribed methods and standard techniques including drawing sequence and presentation, sizing various system components and refining rough sketches.
  • Assist with initial rough designs or sketches and redlines or mark-ups on ongoing projects.
  • Assist with analyzing reports, drawings, and tests, to plan and design projects.
  • Apply design codes and specifications related to design requirements.
  • Complete and review design drawings within scope, budget and schedule.  Perform project tasks and assignments within budgeted hours and deadlines.
  • Incorporate schedules, budgets and project criteria into electrical design and layouts.
  • Organize and maintain all revisions of project drawings, plot files and project-related information in a logical and well-structured manner.
  • Performs other duties as assigned
  • Complies with all policies and standards.

  • Strong understanding of design software such as: AutoCAD, MicroStation, and Excel to develop the design deliverables. 
  • Ability to learn and effectively utilize new design software programs. 
  • Ability to utilize both 2D and 3D applications to prepare engineering drawings. 
  • Basic knowledge in design, calculations and design systems. 
  • Basic knowledge in the theory and practices of engineering discipline; competent in the fundamental concepts of other disciplines as they relate to the assigned discipline; and capable of operating related discipline design software necessary to execute production tasks. 
  • Ability to work collaboratively with others (Project Managers, Engineers and Designers) in a multi-discipline project team environment to resolve design or drawing issues as appropriate and to accomplish objectives.

job.title: Lead Substation Electrical Engineer (34746)
job.description:
Lead Substation Electrical Engineer
Reading, PA (Remote)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)

Summary:
You will provide engineering support for the design of substation facilities for 69kV.

Responsibilities:
  • Substation physical design including overall engineering and analysis for new switchyards and substations, additions and modifications.
  • Preparation of specifications and provide procurement support.
  • Preparation of engineering drawings including layout and general arrangements for all major equipment and structures required for high voltage switchyards or substation.  
  • Drawings include site plans, plan and elevation views, grounding, conduit, lighting, lightning protection and shielding, control building arrangements and details, AC station service, DC systems and Bills of Material. 
  • Perform auxiliary electrical power system studies including load flow, short circuit, voltage drop, protection and coordination, ampacity, and battery sizing studies. 
  • Perform substation grounding calculations and studies, lightning protection and insulation coordination, lighting, bus calculations, transformer and bus sizing calculations, fault calculations, and AC and DC auxiliary system sizing calculations.
  • Perform proposal preparation, proposal estimates, and provide input to scope documents and client presentations.
  • Work in a team-oriented environment with ability to work independently or as part of a group when and as required.
  • Commitment to customer service and ability to work independently on more varied and complex tasks when required.
  • Supervise/coordinate other engineers of equal and lesser experience to successfully deliver a project.
  • Provides technical direction to subordinate team members. 
  • Complete tasks in accordance with project procedures, applicable codes and/or standards, on schedule and within budget.
  • Discuss technical problems and communicate effectively with other project personnel and outside customers.
  • Perform independent audits and reviews of technical work.
  • Perform the function of a team leader that can solve complex issues and act as a lead engineer responsible for the quality of the deliverables. 
  • Interface with other departmental staff, project management, and with outside customers.
  • Ability to travel to job sites to perform site walkdown. 
  • Responsible for the quality assurance of the documents to the applicable drawing standards and responsible for the proper document management procedures are followed.

Requirements:
  • Experience with power system modeling and analysis tools (e.g., ETAP, CYME, SKM).
  • Highly effective technical and practical engineering skills and ability to consistently deliver quality, accurate and timely work to Worley and its customers.
  • A sound knowledge of the fundamental requirements of the electrical engineering, and the basic requirements of other engineering fields.
  • A sound knowledge of applicable local and international standards and statutory and regulatory requirements.
  • Competent use of electrical engineering specific software.
  • Competent use of relevant software such as MS Office Suite.
  • Substantial technical land practical experience in electrical engineering in the same or similar industry including experience in working in multi-disciplinary projects and teams.
  • Experience working in project leadership role.
  • Experience in estimating project costs and writing proposal content.
  • Ideal candidate should have strong interpersonal and written communication skills that permit working efficiently alongside other senior substation engineers and designers in a team setting.
  • Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering and 12 or more years of experience in substation design including supervisory experience.
  • Professional Engineer (PE) in the United States.


We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Mechanical Engineering Manager (34687)
job.description:
Job Title: Engineering Manager

Location: On-site -South Holland, IL
Department: Engineering
Ray Rate: 135-165k

Company Overview

We are a U.S.-based engineering and manufacturing organization specializing in custom-engineered mechanical systems. Our solutions support industries such as energy, defense, aerospace, advanced manufacturing, research, and healthcare.

Operating in an engineer-to-order (ETO) environment, we design and build complex mechanical assemblies, pressure systems, and process equipment that require high levels of precision, compliance, and technical expertise.

Position Summary

The Engineering Manager leads the engineering function and is accountable for the quality, accuracy, and timeliness of all engineering deliverables.

This role ensures that designs:

  • Meet customer requirements

  • Are manufacturable and cost-effective

  • Comply with applicable codes and standards

  • Are delivered on schedule

The Engineering Manager works cross-functionally with Operations, Project Management, Quality, and Sales to drive strong execution in a custom engineered-to-order manufacturing environment.

Key Responsibilities

Leadership & Team Management

  • Lead, mentor, and develop a multidisciplinary team of engineers and design professionals

  • Establish clear priorities, workloads, and performance expectations

  • Conduct performance reviews and support professional development

  • Foster a culture of accountability, collaboration, and technical excellence

Technical Oversight & Quality

  • Lead design reviews and ensure compliance with relevant codes, standards, and customer specifications

  • Oversee engineering calculations, drawings, and documentation

  • Ensure designs are manufacturable and aligned with production capabilities

  • Maintain high standards for accuracy and completeness of engineering output

Process & Continuous Improvement

  • Develop, implement, and continuously improve engineering standards, procedures, and workflows

  • Improve engineering efficiency, documentation control, and change management processes

  • Support configuration management and data control within PDM/PLM systems

Cross-Functional Collaboration

  • Coordinate closely with Production, Quality, and Procurement to ensure seamless handoff into fabrication and assembly

  • Partner with Sales and Operations to support technical proposals and customer engagement

  • Support project management teams to ensure engineering deliverables meet schedule commitments

Requirements

Education & Certifications

  • Bachelor’s degree in Mechanical, Chemical, Industrial, or related Engineering discipline required

  • CAD certification (e.g., SolidWorks Professional) or equivalent experience highly desirable

  • 10+ years of engineering experience in manufacturing, fabrication, or engineered systems environments

  • 5+ years of experience leading an engineering or design team

  • Proven track record delivering engineer-to-order (ETO) or design-build projects

  • Experience working in regulated or code-driven environments preferred

  • High proficiency in 3D CAD software (e.g., SolidWorks), 2D drafting tools, and PDM/PLM systems

  • Working knowledge of pressure systems and applicable industry codes and standards

  • Experience with mechanical systems, process equipment, vacuum systems, cryogenic systems, or pressure vessels preferred

  • Ability to interpret technical requirements and collaborate with internal or third-party code specialists

Leadership & Soft Skills

  • Demonstrated ability to build, lead, and motivate high-performing technical teams

  • Strong organizational and project prioritization skills

  • Excellent verbal and written communication skills

  • Ability to present complex technical information to both technical and non-technical stakeholders

  • Strong problem-solving and decision-making capabilities

job.title: Electrical Engineer (34713)
job.description:
Title: Lead Electrical Engineer
Location: Baton Rouge, LA
Salary: $170,000.00

This person will bring extensive experience to work on industrial projects including design of power distribution, lighting and grounding systems. Projects range from small facility modifications to complex EPC contracts for heavy industrial processes in any one or more of the pulp and paper, chemicals, food and beverage, consumer products, or pharmaceutical industries.  Some travel to project and construction sites will be required.
• Design power system facilities and equipment and coordinate construction, operation, and maintenance of electric power generating, receiving, and distribution stations, and distribution systems and equipment
• Develop conceptual layouts and sketches to drive the most efficient design on a project-by-project basis
• Participate in the planning, scheduling, conducting and coordinating detailed phases of electrical engineering projects
• Develop equipment specifications
• Develop construction specifications and contractor scope of work documents
• Directs preparation of or prepares drawings and specific type of equipment and materials to be used, in construction and equipment installation
• Inspects completed installations for conformance with design and equipment specifications and safety standards
• Work products will include: one-line diagrams, wiring drawings, panel layout drawings, motor schematics, instrument loop sheets, conduit and tray routing drawings and contractor scope documents
• Work with systems and equipment grounding requirements (high resistance, building and isolated grounding)
• Provide on-site start up support
 
YOUR IDEAL BACKGROUND:
The successful candidate should have an entrepreneurial spirit, and be excited by opportunities to add value to our clients with engineering expertise. Strong communication skills, collaboration and courage to challenge the status quo are cornerstones.
• experience with design/installation of medium and low-voltage distribution equipment, with experience in consulting engineering and construction industry
• Strong knowledge of applicable codes and standards (NEC, ANSI, NEMA, NFPA) 
• Able to handle onsite plant projects with minimal supervision
• Well organized with a demonstrated ability to meet deadlines and operate within budgets and work directly with client engineers and maintenance teams.
• Working knowledge of Autodesk Revit.
• Capability with CAD systems including AutoCAD
• PE license or EIT certificate with ability to obtain PE license is preferred but not required.

job.description:
Automation Engineer
Location: Houston, TX
Pay Range: $70.00 - $100.00 per hour
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)

Purpose:

The primary purpose of this role is to drive execution of automation engineering projects within the Autonomous and Automation Americas team by leveraging cutting-edge digital, technology, and autonomous solutions across multiple vendor platforms. The consultant will play a pivotal role in delivering innovative solutions that enhance operational efficiency, safety, and sustainability in mining, on-shore or off-shore operations.

This is a full-time hybrid position based in Houston, Texas, Denver, Colorado, or Calgary, Alberta, Canada.


Overall Responsibilities and Key Accountabilities
  • Strong self-starter, entrepreneurial mindset and motivated.
  • Work with client to share work and integrate staff on projects for a blended execution team.
  • Project experience including coordinating interfaces with engineering partners, construction contractors, equipment manufacturers, system integrators and project management as well as technical disciplines including process, civil, structural, piping, mechanical, and electrical.
  • Desire to expand role and be adaptable to client's needs.
  • Assists, and later, leads the preparation of proposals, execution plans and budgets.
  • Project experience working on all engineering phases within a project from concept through design and engineering, construction support, and commissioning/validation and handover.
  • Programming and configuring PLC and DCS platforms while leading a team for project implementation. Hands on and leading.
  • Team will be responsible for producing process automation deliverables - control narratives, cause and effect drawings, Boolean logic diagrams, DCS / SIS / PLC functional specifications, produce system cabinet layout drawings, wiring diagrams, system card layout and loading.  Also, basic I&C deliverables such as instrument datasheets, instrument index, wiring diagrams, system architecture will be in scope.
  • Project experience in the development and commissioning of complex automation projects.
  • Excellent communication skills including ability to communicate complex ideas effectively, produce concise reports and presentations appropriate for all levels.
  • Takes ownership for project success: delivery of target quality, cost management and customer satisfaction.
  • Proactively builds a knowledge base of tools and methodologies to drive productivity and growth.
  • Lead role in work process improvement.
  • Ability to work collaboratively in a multicultural environment.
  • Travel as required for various field programs – can be 25-50% depending on project phase and assignment.
  • Experience working with Honeywell automation systems, especially in industrial/process environments.
  • Rockwell Experience.
  • Yokogawa experience is a plus to have.
  • Emerson experience is a plus to have.

job.title: Field Civil Engineer (34740)
job.description:
Field Civil Engineer  
Charlotte, NC (On-site)
Salary Range: $80,000 – $100,000
Per Diem: $105/day

Overview
Our client, a well-established civil engineering organization, is seeking a Field Civil Engineer to work on active construction sites in North Carolina. This hands-on role involves verifying construction against engineering plans, assisting with structural element placement, and collaborating closely with site crews. The ideal candidate thrives in a field-based environment and has strong technical and problem-solving skills.

Key Responsibilities
  • Plan, design, and manage permits for civil engineering and infrastructure projects
  • Conduct site visits to ensure construction aligns with design specifications
  • Prepare and modify engineering documents including reports, plans, schedules, and cost estimates using AutoCAD, Civil 3D, and other design software
  • Establish survey control and lay out secondary control points for building elements
  • Assess site conditions using maps, drawings, and geotechnical data to guide project planning
  • Recommend process improvements to increase efficiency and quality
  • Apply advanced engineering techniques and mathematics to project challenges
  • Collaborate with team members to complete project tasks and assignments

Qualifications
  • Bachelor’s degree in Civil Engineering
  • Minimum 4 years of site civil engineering experience
  • Engineer-in-Training (EIT) certification; working toward Professional Engineer licensure
  • Proficiency in AutoCAD and Civil 3D
  • Experience with grading, earthwork, and stormwater drainage design (Autodesk SSA)
  • Knowledge of environmental permitting (NPDES, wetlands, Phase 1 ESA)
  • Site layout experience, including parking, access, roadways, and ADA compliance
  • Roadway/paving design and specifications knowledge
  • Surveying and geotechnical knowledge relevant to site preparation and heavy haul paving
  • Strong verbal and written communication skills
Why Candidates Are Interested in This Role
  • Opportunity to work on active, large-scale civil construction projects
  • Hands-on field experience with ownership of critical site tasks
  • Professional growth toward PE licensure
  • Exposure to a wide range of civil engineering disciplines and technical tools
  • Competitive salary and daily per diem

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. job.title: Senior Electrical Engineer (34698)
job.description:
Senior Electrical Engineer 
Downers Grove, IL (On-site)

Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $67.40 - $84.14 

Summary: Looking for a talented and self-motivated Lead Electrical Engineer with power plant design experience to help Lead projects. This is a great opportunity to get involved on challenging and interesting design projects within the Power industry.

Responsibilities:
  • Work on a major project or several projects of moderate scope with complex designs.
  • Plan and schedule team activities.
  • Estimate specifications, requisition, calculation, and quantities for proposed projects.
  • Develop budget requirements (i.e., time and materials) for proposed projects.
  • Monitor and manage team performance and budget compliance.
  • Develop and forecast staffing requirements and quantities for installation.
  • Lead a team of engineers and designers in order to complete assignments on projects.
  • Assigns and schedule tasks to and direct engineers, designers, and administrative staff to meet project schedules.
  • Coordinate project activities across multiple office locations.
  • Coordinate design and engineering activities with other engineering disciplines.
  • Reviews complete project documents for conformity and quality assurance while assuring adherence to applicable codes, standards, and project requirements.
  • Conceptually identify interface requirements for existing facilities.
  • Participate in constructability reviews for assigned project.
  • Develops new techniques and/or improved processes, materials, or products.
  • Provide support to field construction and commissioning activities.
  • Monitor and report on project engineering discipline status to Project Manager/Leadership Team.
  • Participate in client meetings.
  • Assists upper-level management and staff as a technical specialist or advisor.
  • Mentor younger professionals, leading by example.
  • Design guidance for raceway systems including underground ductbanks, cable tray and above ground conduit.
  • Design guidance for lighting systems including interior, exterior and roadway lighting.
  • Design guidance for grounding systems.
  • Develop schematics and wiring diagrams.
  • Develop one-line and three-line diagrams.
  • Coordination with engineering and other crafts to assure a complete, coordinated design.
Requirements:
  • Bachelor’s degree in electrical engineering from a 4 year ABET accredited college.
  • Minimum of 7 years of electrical engineering design experience on Power and/or Industrial Projects.
  • Professional Engineering License with the ability to obtain licensure in other states within the US.
  • Prior Lead Engineer experience with executing projects.
  • Working knowledge of the National Electrical Code, IEEE/ANSI Standards and NEMA Standards.
  • Experience with Medium and Low Voltage Equipment and Cables.
  • Must be able to understand and comply with company policies, procedures, and processes.
  • Must possess excellent verbal and written communication skills with the ability to interface with Senior Company Leadership.


We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Project Controls Specialist (34706)
job.description:
Project Controls Specialist
Chicago, IL
Pay Range: $52.00 - $69.00 / hr
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, & 401(k)
 
Description
 The Project Controls Manager is responsible for providing project leadership with accurate and timely information related to quantities, earned value progress, cost, forecasts, and schedule performance to support strong decision-making. Oversee major project control functions including Planning, Cost, Scheduling, and Change Management to ensure consistent implementation of corporate processes, reports, and systems.


Responsibilities
  • Ensure project set-up in the Enterprise Planning & Controls Software.
  • Analyze data, review logs, and run reports in the Enterprise Planning & Controls Software or other tools.
  • Develop, implement, and maintain effective scheduling reports, cost reports, cash flows and forecasts for multiple projects.
  • Responsible for engineering, procurement and construction planning, scheduling, logistics and cost control.
  • Serves as a point of contact for technical questions from the project team.
  • Approve Purchase Orders and/or Subcontracts for proper coding.
  • Input and document changes to cost reports or forecasts.
  • Interfaces with the Project Management Team and Accounting Department to review project costs and contingency costs and support external audits.
  • Develop various levels of schedules and consult with the project team on schedule progress.
  • Analyzes and evaluates schedule management involving the critical path method of scheduling techniques, estimating, project cost management, and forecasting.
Requirements
  • 3+ years of direct project controls experience requires. 
  • Bachelor Degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or related field. Applicable experience may be substituted for the degree requirement.
  • Oracle Primavera P6 experience required
  • Proficient with Microsoft Office.
  • Strong analytical and problem solving skills.
  • Excellent interpersonal skills.

We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Senior HVAC Hydronics Mechanical Engineer (34727)
job.description:
Senior HVAC / Hydronics Mechanical Engineer (PE)
100% Remote
Contract to Direct
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, and 401(k)

Seeking a highly experienced, hands-on HVAC / Hydronics Mechanical Engineer (PE) with deep expertise in heat transfer and full-system cooling design. This individual will lead the design of complex mechanical systems from central plant generation through distribution and end-use HVAC equipment across industrial and commercial projects.

Responsibilities

  • Design complete cooling systems including cooling towers, chiller plants, pumping systems, and terminal HVAC equipment.
  • Perform detailed heat transfer calculations, equipment sizing, and system optimization.
  • Size and specify cooling towers, chillers, pumps, heat exchangers, pot feeders, filters, AHUs, DEHUs, RAHs, DOAS, RTUs and CRAH/CRAC units
  • Data center experience preferred
  • Perform pipe sizing and duct sizing calculations.
  • Develop material specifications and equipment selections.
  • Ensure compliance with applicable codes and standards including ASHRAE, SMACNA, IMC, and relevant building codes.
  • Coordinate with vendors and equipment manufacturers.
Qualifications
  • Licensed Professional Engineer (PE) with NCEES record.
  • Ability to obtain licensure in multiple states.
  • Extensive experience in HVAC hydronic system design, chiller plants, and industrial ventilation systems.
  • Strong knowledge of mechanical codes and industry standards.
  • Proven ability to independently design, size, and specify complete mechanical systems.
  • Dust collection system design experience is preferred
  • VOC abatement systems, including specification of thermal oxidizers is preferred
  • Experience working with dust collection vendors to design and implement collection systems.
We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics.

job.title: Structural Revit Designer (34714)
job.description:
Structural Design Technician Career Level 
Hybrid - role almost any US state
Benefits: Standard Medical, Dental, 401(k)
Pay Range: $32.69 /hr. - $44.23 /hr.

Summary:
As a Career Level Structural Designer, you will continue to apply your expertise as a key contributor to the design team, responsible for developing design concepts and producing 2D and 3D deliverables. As part of this team; you will be responsible for developing conceptual and detailed structural models that support advanced technology manufacturing environments, including semiconductor fabrication and support facilities. 

Responsibilities:
  • Proficient in the development of 3D structural models based on verbal instructions or rough sketches.
  • Produce complete sets of structural plans and details.
  • Apply department and client standards consistently across models and documents.
  • Take full ownership of assigned scope and meet schedule and quality requirements.
  • Highly proficient in verifying completeness and accuracy of own work.
  • Coordinate design efforts with related disciplines.
  • Support team members by addressing needs.
  • Advance design automation skills and embrace emerging technologies.
  • Review models and documents for quality and compliance.
  • Ability to lead small design teams:
    • Provide leadership for small packages and guide a small team of designers.
    • Coordinate team tasks, balance workloads, monitor progress to ensure deadlines are obtained, and ensure quality standards including clash adjudicated design.
    • Take ownership of internal reviews and lead cross-discipline and external package reviews.
    • Attend meetings and actively help resolve concerns and solve problems.
  • Contribute to a collaborative team environment and uphold a Culture of Care.
Qualifications:
  • Associate degree in Computer-Aided Drafting or equivalent.
  • Minimum of 5 years drafting/design experience in the structural field.
  • Minimum of 3 years Revit experience.
  • Strong interpersonal leadership skills and communication skills.


We are an Equal Opportunity Employer and do not discriminate on the basis of race, color, religion, sex, national origin, age, disability, veteran status, genetic information, sexual orientation, gender identity, or any other legally protected status. All qualified applicants will receive consideration for employment without regard to any of these characteristics. #STSIEG

job.title: Southeast Regional Sales Representative (34128)
job.description:
Job Title: Regional Sales Representative
Job Type: Direct Hire
Location: 100% Remote in Virginia, Tennessee, North Carolina, South Carolina, Georgia, Alabama, Mississippi, Florida
Travel: Up to 25% to visit clients and attend meetings
Base Salary: $95,000- $100,000 + commission
Company Overview
A manufacturer and installer of industrial spiral and tunnel freezer systems serving food manufacturers and processors across multiple markets. The company provides freezing solutions designed to improve operational efficiency, product quality, and production capacity.
Position Summary
This role is responsible for selling capital equipment, specifically large industrial freezer systems, to food manufacturers across the Southeast. The Regional Sales Representative will receive inbound leads and manage an existing book of business, serving as the primary point of contact for customers looking to expand or improve their freezing operations.
Approximately 65% of the role focuses on growing existing accounts and converting inbound leads while maintaining strong relationships through regular client meetings, on-site visits, and facility tours. The remaining 35% focuses on new business development, including generating new leads through cold calls and networking events.
The representative assesses customer needs, prepares quotes and proposals, and guides customers through the buying process while coordinating with internal engineering, project management, and production teams. The role also includes tracking activity in a CRM and meeting assigned sales targets.
Key Responsibilities
  • Identify and engage food manufacturing facilities and processing plants
  • Build relationships with plant leadership and purchasing decision-makers
  • Assess customer needs related to freezing capacity and production requirements
  • Recommend appropriate industrial freezer solutions
  • Prepare quotes and proposals and guide customers through the buying process
  • Coordinate with internal engineering, project management, and production teams
  • Track leads, opportunities, and customer interactions in a CRM system
  • Travel to customer sites and job locations as needed
  • Close sales and meet performance targets
Qualifications
  • High school diploma (minimum)
  • 10–15 years of sales experience
  • Experience in B2B or capital equipment sales (food processing equipment preferred)
  • Familiarity with food manufacturing or industrial processing environments
  • Strong communication, negotiation, and technical sales skills
  • Ability to manage long sales cycles and multiple opportunities
  • Self-motivated with strong organizational and time-management skills
  • Proficiency with standard business software and CRM tools
  • Willingness to travel up to 25%
